$note: Surface-lens layer for the misconception canvas (Smith's favourite prism, P3; reporting_prisms thread). A LENS is a learner-facing grouping over the 17 families - the misconception twin of vocab themes over lemmas, and analyst-owned exactly as Vocab owns its chip list. A lens is computed as: event.misconception in this lens's member set. Lenses MAY overlap (a specific can sit in more than one), by design. Each lens is ALSO a Special Drill (its member set as a session filter). Rendered in Linguics house style (serif, cream, navy, CEFR tints, green/blue cells) not generic CDS. members reference the 75 ratified ids; pending_extension_members await the pass-1 extension ratification (harvest v15).
governance: analyst proposes this cut; Architecture ratifies; Housing renders it, inventing nothing.

lenses.label: Accents and silent letters
lenses.learner_blurb: the little marks that change the word
lenses.within_lens_drill: by host: verb / noun / monosyllable / preposition+article
lenses.the_sentence: You drop written accents across the board, not in one topic.
pending_extension_members: orthography added-accent / hypercorrection (Housing classifies this at runtime as orthography.accent.added; belongs to this lens, not spelling)

lenses.label: Spelling that tracks sound
lenses.learner_blurb: small spelling slips where the letters follow the sound
lenses.within_lens_drill: by rule: hard c/g h-insertion / double-i / -cia--gia / -iare stress / geminate / elision
lenses.the_sentence: Your spelling errors are the sound-preserving ones (cerchi, mangi, dammi), not random typos.
overlaps_with: orthography.failed_elision + gemination.enclitic_doubling also appear in pronoun_machinery

lenses.label: Word order
lenses.within_lens_drill: by structure: adjective / clitic / negation / adverb
lenses.the_sentence: You place words in the English order where Italian differs.

lenses.label: Agreement
lenses.learner_blurb: making words match in gender, number and person
lenses.within_lens_drill: by locus: adjective / participle / article (prenominal) / clitic / subject-verb; and by feature: gender vs number vs person
lenses.the_sentence: Your agreement slips show up on adjectives, participles, articles AND clitics alike.

lenses.label: Building irregular forms
lenses.learner_blurb: making the awkward forms the language actually uses
lenses.within_lens_drill: by tense; by conjugation class (-are / -ere / -ire); by individual irregular verb
lenses.the_sentence: You regularise the irregulars - the exact forms that need memorising.

lenses.label: Choosing the right form
lenses.learner_blurb: picking the right tense, mood or word for the meaning
lenses.within_lens_drill: by contrast: tense choice / mood trigger / aspect / auxiliary
lenses.the_sentence: When two forms are both well-built, you pick the wrong one for the context.
pending_extension_members: paradigm_confusion.preposition_choice, paradigm_confusion.relative_choice, paradigm_confusion.connective_choice (extension) - the 'which word' choices

lenses.label: English (or L1) interference
lenses.learner_blurb: when English (or Spanish, French) leaks in
lenses.within_lens_drill: by construction: calque / clitic omission / foreign plural / word-order calque
lenses.the_sentence: A cluster of your errors are direct mappings from another language.
pending_extension_members: transfer.foreign_s_plural, transfer.construction_nonfinite variants (extension); note the family definition is being broadened past English-only

lenses.label: Pronoun machinery
lenses.learner_blurb: the little words - clitics, ci, ne, glielo
lenses.within_lens_drill: by aspect: position / cluster form / case (DOP vs IOP) / gender-number / omission
lenses.the_sentence: Your pronoun trouble is [position / cluster / case], not pronouns-in-general.
overlaps_with: deliberately cross-family: this lens COLLECTS the pronoun surface that the family axis scatters across clitic_cluster, word_order, paradigm_confusion, agreement, orthography, gemination and transfer. It is the clearest demonstration of why the lens layer exists.

notes_for_architecture: Overlap is intentional (a specific in two lenses). If you want lenses to PARTITION instead, say so and I will assign each specific to exactly one - but I recommend overlap, matching vocab themes.

Each lens doubles as a Special Drill (member set as session filter) - but per Smith's steer the immediate drill need is just more basic formation ITEMS from the verb authors, so treat drill-generation as a later free consequence, not a blocker.
The within_lens_drill axis is the SKILL REGION (where it fired), which every event already carries - so the click-through works today wherever tagging exists.
false_friend, register, stress are not their own lens (too small); stress rides accents-or-building, register rides choosing, false_friend is reserved.
